key_insight: Materials-science mechanisms in an open-weight language model are encoded not only in readable hidden states, but also in controlled state transformations that track physical laws and can causally steer answers.
engineering_relevance: This suggests LLMs can be probed and intervened on to inspect, orient, and potentially control mechanistic reasoning for materials-design workflows.
method: Matched direct and Jacobian vocabulary readouts from hidden states
Option-free state-geometry analysis plus counterfactual benchmark of 60 laws
Causal interventions and state patches on answer probabilities
evidence_signal: Across 50 held-out descriptions, three Jacobian lenses reproduced concept ranks; 9 of 10 mechanism families were identified, 39 of 40 directional laws were oriented correctly, and interventions shifted 12 matched cases.
watch_item: Some apparent physical structure in hidden-state neighborhoods was also explained by numerical comparison, so representational claims need careful control analyses before being interpreted as true physics understanding.
paper_title: Reading and Steering Representations of Materials-Science Mechanisms in an Open-Weight Language Model
